content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听，并回答第21至第26题。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 26.

content.cn_text: Lecturer：好的，你们想在班里组织某种正式讨论，对吗，Daniel？
content.en_text: Lecturer: Right now, you wanted to organize a formal discussion of some sort in the class. Is that right, Daniel?

content.cn_text: Daniel：是的，我们觉得用一场正式辩论来结束心理学课程会很好。
content.en_text: Daniel: Yes, we thought it would be really good to end our psychology course with a formal debate.

content.cn_text: 就是提出一个陈述，然后大家支持或反对它。
content.en_text: You know, where there's a statement and people argue for or against it.

content.cn_text: Lecturer：好主意。你们的热情从哪里来？你们有人以前组织过辩论吗？
content.en_text: Lecturer: Good idea. Where did the enthusiasm come from? Have either of you organized debates before?
Fiona? Fiona？

content.cn_text: Fiona：没有，但我的一个朋友在中学最后一年组织过，他说自己从中收获很多。
content.en_text: Fiona: No, but one of my friends did in his last year at secondary school and he says he got a lot out of doing it.

content.cn_text: 而且我知道这所大学的很多其他院系也有辩论。
content.en_text: And I know they have debates in lots of other departments here at the university.

content.cn_text: Lecturer：明白。那你觉得学生们，也就是你们和其他人，会从中得到什么？
content.en_text: Lecturer: I see. So what do you think the students, you and the others, would get out of this?

content.cn_text: Fiona：辩论会鼓励大家研究一个主题的各个方面。
content.en_text: Fiona: Well, a debate will encourage people to study all sides of the subject matter.

content.cn_text: 我还认为，听别人的观点能帮助你更聚焦自己的想法。
content.en_text: And I also think listening to other people's ideas can have a focusing effect on your own.

content.cn_text: 有时候，直到你听到自己不同意的东西，你才会知道自己真正相信什么。
content.en_text: Sometimes you kind of don't know what you truly believe until you hear something you disagree with.

content.cn_text: Lecturer：好的，那辩论题目呢？
content.en_text: Lecturer: Okay, what about the topic for the debate?

content.cn_text: Daniel:我们有三个可选题目。等一下给你看，确认它们是否合适。
content.en_text: Daniel: We've got three to choose from. We'll show you in a minute to check they're okay.

content.cn_text: 一开始，我们想让其他学生提建议。
content.en_text: At first, we thought we'd ask the other students to suggest something.

content.cn_text: 但我们担心会得到30个不同题目。
content.en_text: But we were afraid we'd get 30 different topics.

content.cn_text: 所以我们两个自己头脑风暴了一些题目，然后列出候选名单。
content.en_text: So instead, we brainstormed some, just the two of us, then made a shortlist.

content.cn_text: 然后我们会让大家投票选出最喜欢的一个。
content.en_text: And we'll get them to vote for the one they like best.

content.cn_text: Lecturer：好的。
content.en_text: Lecturer: Right.

content.cn_text: Daniel：但问题是，如果大家不知道辩论如何进行，他们可能会觉得处于劣势。
content.en_text: Daniel: But the thing is, if people don't know how debates work, they could feel at a disadvantage.

content.cn_text: 我的意思是，这不会影响他们的成绩之类的，但可能会让他们却步。
content.en_text: I mean, it wouldn't affect their grades or anything, but it could put them off.

content.cn_text: Fiona: 所以我们觉得应该提前开个会，确保大家清楚辩论涉及什么。
content.en_text: Fiona: So we thought we should have a meeting beforehand to make sure everyone's clear about what's involved in a debate.

content.cn_text: 以及如何准备。
content.en_text: And how to prepare for it.

content.cn_text: Lecturer：我觉得这是个好主意。
content.en_text: Lecturer: I think that's a good idea.

content.cn_text: Fiona：我们还想在那里明确一场成功辩论的一些关键点。
content.en_text: Fiona: And we thought we could establish some key points of a successful debate there.

content.cn_text: 比如，辩论是思考一个问题的有效方式。
content.en_text: Like that debates are effective methods for considering an issue.

content.cn_text: 还要理解一个论点的双方，才能有效支持其中一方。
content.en_text: And that you need to understand both sides of an argument in order to support one side effectively.

content.cn_text: 我们觉得最好使用一个学生们已经有强烈观点的问题。
content.en_text: We think it'll be best if we can use an issue that the students already have strong opinions about.

content.cn_text: Daniel: 这样有些人会愿意支持它，另一些人会愿意反对它。
content.en_text: Daniel: Then some people will be happy to speak in support of it and others to oppose it.

content.cn_text: Lecturer：对。
content.en_text: Lecturer: Right.

content.cn_text: 你们还想在会议上做什么？
content.en_text: What else do you want to do at the meeting?

content.cn_text: Daniel：我们得考虑大家如何为辩论做背景研究。
content.en_text: Daniel: Well, we've got to think about how people will do the background research for the debate.

content.cn_text: 我们可以把所有人分成两个团队。
content.en_text: We could organize everyone into two teams.

content.cn_text: 这样应该可以为主要发言人节省时间。他们不必独自完成。
content.en_text: That should save time for the main speakers. They won't have to do it on their own.

content.cn_text: 我们还需要确定一些规则。
content.en_text: And we'll need to decide on some rules.

content.cn_text: 比如每个人发言多久。
content.en_text: Like how long each person speaks for.

content.cn_text: 还要选一名辩论主席，要非常自信的人。
content.en_text: And to select a chair for the debate. Someone who's really confident.

content.cn_text: Lecturer：好。
content.en_text: Lecturer: Fine.

content.cn_text: 现在，我觉得辩论之后应该有某种后续任务。
content.en_text: Now, I think you should have some sort of follow-up to the debate.

content.cn_text: 辩论结束后，我希望你们让每个人写一篇短文，重点写他们从参与中获得了什么。
content.en_text: After it's over, I'd like you to get everyone to write a short essay focusing on what they gain from participating in it.

content.cn_text: 反过来，这也可以和我们一直在做的学习与动机相关内容结合起来。
content.en_text: And in turn, that can feed into the work we've been doing on learning and motivation.

content.cn_text: Fiona：但你愿意阅读这些短文并反馈吗？我的意思是，给一些意见。
content.en_text: Fiona: But would you be willing to read them and report back? I mean, give some feedback on them.

content.cn_text: Lecturer：如果我不愿意，就不会提出这个建议了。
content.en_text: Lecturer: I wouldn't have made the suggestion if I wasn't.

content.cn_text: Fiona：谢谢。
content.en_text: Fiona: Thanks.

content.cn_text: 在听剩余讨论之前，你有一些时间浏览第27至第30题。
content.en_text: Before you hear the rest of the discussion, you have some time to look at questions 27 to 30.

content.cn_text: 现在请听录音，并回答第27至第30题。
content.en_text: Now listen and answer questions 27 to 30.

content.cn_text: Lecturer：好的，现在我们再多想想那个会议。
content.en_text: Lecturer: Okay, now let's think a bit more about the meeting.

content.cn_text: 你们需要明确决定如何向学生介绍辩论的不同特征。
content.en_text: You need to decide exactly how you'll introduce the students to different features of a debate.

content.cn_text: Daniel：首先，我们需要告诉大家辩论中实际会发生什么，比如程序等等。
content.en_text: Daniel: Well, first of all, we need to inform everyone about what actually happens at a debate. The procedures and so on.

content.cn_text: 我们可以讲解一下，并把主要信息写在黑板上。
content.en_text: We could talk about that and write the main information on the board.

content.cn_text: Fiona：或者，不那样做，我们可以总结主要阶段，然后发给大家。
content.en_text: Fiona: Or instead of that, we could make a summary of the main stages and hand that out.

content.cn_text: 然后再讲解。 是的。好，这样更好。
content.en_text: Daniel: Then talk it through. Yes. Okay, that's better.

content.cn_text: Fiona：好的，但我们也需要谈谈肢体语言。
content.en_text: Fiona: Okay, but we do need to talk about body language as well.

content.cn_text: Lecturer：是的，那是非常重要的工具。我有一篇很有意思的相关文章，里面有一些关于眼神交流等技巧的好图示。
content.en_text: Lecturer: Yes, that's a very important tool. I've got an interesting article on that with some good diagrams of techniques for things like eye contact.

content.cn_text: 我会复印一些，让你们发出去。 Fiona：太好了。
content.en_text: I'll make some copies for you to give out. Fiona: Great.

content.cn_text: Daniel: 然后我们需要考虑大家在哪里找到辩论主题的信息。
content.en_text: Daniel: Then we need to think about where people will find the information on the debate topic.

content.cn_text: 所以他们需要知道去哪里找信息。
content.en_text: So they'll need to know where to look for the information.

content.cn_text: 我们可以整理一份资料来源清单，比如教材和文章等。
content.en_text: We could compile a list of sources, textbooks and articles and so on.

content.cn_text: Fiona：可以，但大家不能在会议上提出自己的建议和意见吗？
content.en_text: Fiona: We could, but couldn't people offer their own suggestions and advice on that at the meeting instead?

content.cn_text: Lecturer：可以。让所有人都参与进来。Daniel: 对。
content.en_text: Lecturer: Yes. Get everyone involved. Daniel: Right.

content.cn_text: Fiona: 那关于提问和回答问题的准备呢？
content.en_text: Fiona: What about preparation for asking and answering questions?

content.cn_text: 我们可以试着演示一下，但我不确定怎么做。
content.en_text: We could try to demonstrate that, but I'm not sure how.

content.cn_text: Daniel: 也许我们可以在黑板上列出要点，然后讨论。
content.en_text: Daniel: Maybe we could put up a list of points on the board. Then discuss them.

content.cn_text: Fiona：是的，这听起来更好。谢谢。
content.en_text: Fiona: Yes, that sounds better. Thanks.

content.cn_text: Lecturer: 好的。那我们再约时间见面。
content.en_text: Lecturer: Okay. Well, let's meet up again.

content.cn_text: 第三部分到此结束。
content.en_text: That is the end of part three.

content.cn_text: 你现在有30秒时间检查第三部分的答案。
content.en_text: You now have 30 seconds to check your answers to part three.

content.cn_text: 第四部分。
content.en_text: Part four.

content.cn_text: 你将听到一名化学专业学生做关于紫色以及紫色染料发展的演讲。
content.en_text: You will hear a chemistry student giving a presentation on the color purple and the development of purple dyes.

content.cn_text: 首先，你有一些时间浏览第31至第40题。
content.en_text: First, you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

groups.desc: Choose the correct letter, A, B, or C.
groups.title: Questions 21–26

items.title: Why is Fiona keen to organise a debate?

options.title: A
options.content: She has attended debates in other departments.

options.title: B
options.content: She organised debates while at secondary school.

options.title: C
options.content: She has talked about debating with a friend.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第21题问 Fiona 为什么热衷于组织辩论。原文说她的朋友组织过并且收获很多，所以答案是 C. She has talked about debating with a friend。其他院系有辩论只是补充信息。

items.title: Fiona says that an advantage of debating is that it can
items.analysis: 答案解析：第22题问 Fiona/Daniel 认为辩论的优势是什么。原文说听到不同观点后才知道自己真正相信什么，说明辩论能帮助学生形成观点，所以答案是 A. help students to form opinions。

items.title: How will the debate topic be selected?

options.title: A
options.content: Daniel and Fiona will ask the lecturer to choose one from a list.

options.title: B
options.content: The other students will select one of Daniel and Fiona’s suggestions.

options.title: C
options.content: Daniel and Fiona will collect suggestions from the other students.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第23题问辩论题目如何选择。原文说 Daniel 和 Fiona 先列出 shortlist，再让其他学生投票选，所以答案是 B. The other students will select one of Daniel and Fiona’s suggestions。

items.title: Fiona and Daniel know the key to a successful debate is for students to

options.title: A
options.content: choose a topic that really involves them.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第24题问成功辩论的关键是什么。原文说最好选择学生已经有 strong opinions 的议题，也就是能真正让他们投入的主题，所以答案是 A. choose a topic that really involves them。

items.title: Daniel thinks it would be good for the students to be in two teams because it would

options.title: A
options.content: give everyone a chance to speak.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第25题问 Daniel 认为把学生分成两个团队有什么好处。原文说 main speakers won't have to do it on their own，即工作量可以分担，所以答案是 C. share the workload between students。

items.title: What does the lecturer agree to do after the debate?

options.title: A
options.content: make comments on students’ written work.

options.title: B
options.content: help Daniel and Fiona to write a report.

options.title: C
options.content: set the students an essay on a different topic.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第26题问导师同意辩论后做什么。原文说愿意 read them and report back，即对学生的书面作业给反馈，所以答案是 A. make comments on students’ written work。

groups.desc: Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A–F, next to questions 27–30.
groups.title: Questions 27–30

list.title: B
list.content: give a handout of key points

list.title: D
list.content: provide a photocopy of an article

items.title: understanding debating procedures
items.analysis: 答案解析：第27题问帮助学生理解 debating procedures 会采取什么行动。原文最后采纳 make a summary and hand that out，所以答案是 B. give a handout of key points。write on the board 是被替代的方案。

items.title: using body language
items.analysis: 答案解析：第28题问帮助学生使用 body language 会采取什么行动。原文说导师会复印一篇有图示的文章让他们发，所以答案是 D. provide a photocopy of an article。

items.title: accessing resources
items.analysis: 答案解析：第29题问帮助学生 accessing resources 会采取什么行动。原文最后决定让大家 offer their own suggestions and advice，所以答案是 A. ask for suggestions。推荐教材/资料清单是先前想法，未采用。

items.title: asking and answering questions
items.analysis: 答案解析：第30题问帮助学生 asking and answering questions 会采取什么行动。原文最终采用 put up a list of points on the board，所以答案是 C. write on the board。demonstrate 被否定。
